findings.message: record_raw_path() captures the raw full_path (including the query string) straight into a metric with no fail-closed query-param allowlist and no sanitization. This is exactly the beacon field the route at routes/frontend_rum.py:46 re-applies the allowlist to strip — this new path bypasses it, trusting whatever a non-browser client POSTs.
findings.suggestion: Strip the query string to the bare pathname and route the value through the server-side frontend_rum_allowed_query_params allowlist (as the existing page-view/attribution recorders do) before it ever reaches a label.

findings.message: app_frontend_raw_path_total emits one series per raw full path (path="<full_path incl. query>") with no distinct-value cap and no "other" overflow bucket. Every unique path/query combination becomes a new Prometheus series — unbounded label cardinality that will blow up Prometheus, unlike the existing route-keyed page_views series which strip the query string.
findings.suggestion: Label by a fixed/sanitized route enum (templated path, query removed) and enforce a distinct-value cap with an "other" overflow bucket, mirroring the bounded labels used for app_frontend_page_views_total.

findings.message: app_frontend_raw_path_total is a new RUM signal scraped via /metrics but ships with no corresponding alert rule or dashboard panel — the signal is unobservable to operators even once emitted.
findings.suggestion: Either add an alert/dashboard panel for the new counter or, preferably, drop this redundant raw-path series in favor of the existing bounded page-view metrics.
